sourceMovie.title: Extreme Job
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Extreme Job for the same reason it broke through in 2019: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Lee Byeong-heon keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 2 Guns
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Extreme Job clicked for you, 2 Guns (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Extreme Job?
faq.answer: Try 2 Guns, Turner & Hooch, K-9—each shares DNA with Extreme Job while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Extreme Job?
faq.answer: Extreme Job (2019) is best described as Action, Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Extreme Job worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
